**Mixed QS5 and 7500 Fast qPCR example**



**This example demonstrates how to combine qPCR result files generated by Applied Biosystems QuantStudio 5 and 7500 Fast instruments into one standardised Excel workbook.**



**Input folders**



**The combined script reads instrument files from two separate folders:**



**QS5 files: examples/qpcr/qs5/input/**

**7500 Fast files: examples/qpcr/fast7500/input/**



**The files may use .xlsx, .xls, or .xlsm formats, provided they contain a worksheet named Results.**



**Run the example**



**From the repository root, run:**



**python scripts\\compile\_mixed\_qpcr.py `**

&#x20;   **--fast7500-input "examples\\qpcr\\fast7500\\input" `**

&#x20;   **--qs5-input "examples\\qpcr\\qs5\\input" `**

&#x20;   **--output "examples\\qpcr\\mixed\_qs5\_fast7500\\expected\_output\\combined\_qpcr.xlsx"**



**The same command can be entered on one line:**



**python scripts\\compile\_mixed\_qpcr.py --fast7500-input "examples\\qpcr\\fast7500\\input" --qs5-input "examples\\qpcr\\qs5\\input" --output "examples\\qpcr\\mixed\_qs5\_fast7500\\expected\_output\\combined\_qpcr.xlsx"**

**Expected output**



**The generated workbook is saved as:**



**expected\_output/combined\_qpcr.xlsx**



**The combined output contains the following columns:**



**Well**

**Sample Name**

**Target Name**

**Task**

**Reporter**

**Ct**

**Date**

**Test method**

**Plate number**

**Instrument**

**Operator**

**Source File**



**Text values of Undetermined in the Ct column are converted to 40.**



**Filename convention**



**Input filenames should follow this structure:**



**YYYYMMDD\_TestMethod\_PlateNumber\_Instrument\_Operator.ext**



**Example:**



**20231214\_test\_p01\_7500fast-instr1\_op4.xls**



**Filename components are used to populate the Date, Test method, Plate number, Instrument and Operator columns.**



**Data status**



**The files included in this repository should be anonymised, synthetic, or approved for public release. Do not upload confidential, clinical, personal, or proprietary data.**
